name: x402
tagline: Sets up pay-per-use Browser Use Cloud access via x402 crypto payments (USDC on Base): wallet creation, funding, and a $1 test run.
verdict: Exceptionally thorough, deterministic, failure-aware scripted flow with good secret discipline (gitignore checks, no echo-back). Dinged for offering wallet-private-key chat paste as a listed option, unpinned crypto/SDK installs, an unchecked overwrite risk when regenerating a wallet, an oversized description, and no versioning signal.

highlights.summary: 'Paste it here' lets the user paste their wallet private key into chat; mitigated by never echoing it, a .gitignore check, and a prominently offered out-of-band alternative.
pip/npm installs (eth-account, viem, browser-use-sdk[x402]) are unpinned; well-known packages, so warning not fail.
Path C (fresh wallet) writes BROWSER_USE_X402_PRIVATE_KEY to .env with no check for a pre-existing key, risking silent overwrite of a funded wallet on re-run.
